title: Jayson Tatum will miss Celtics game against Thunder
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum will miss the Celtics game against the Thunder to manage his right Achilles injury.
The Celtics are 2-1 since Tatum's return and currently hold a 43-22 record in the Eastern Conference.
Payton Pritchard will return from neck spasms, while Derrick White is questionable with a right knee contusion.

title: Boston City Council moves toward potential kratom ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council drafting ordinance to ban kratom, which mimics opioids and stimulants, amid health concerns.
Public health officials worry about synthetic kratom's 7-hydroxymitragynine compound and its contamination risks.
Over 20 Massachusetts municipalities have banned kratom; state-level regulation is under consideration.

title: ‘I was overcome’: Conan O’Brien tells Jimmy Kimmel about ’emotional’ trip to Ireland
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Conan O'Brien visited his great-grandfather's former home in Galbally, Ireland, and described the experience as 'emotional' and 'powerful.'
O'Brien discussed the trip on 'Jimmy Kimmel Live!' ahead of hosting the Oscars, reflecting on his 100% Irish heritage.
He empathized with immigrants' struggles, saying the visit helped him understand the challenges faced by his great-grandfather.

title: MBTA bus crashes into parked cars, home in Medford
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: An MBTA bus crashed into several parked cars and a home on Playstead Road in Medford early Sunday morning.
The bus driver reported losing control due to 'black ice', leading to the collision with two parked vehicles.
MBTA Transit Police confirmed the incident is under investigation but have not provided further details.

title: Here are the richest people in Massachusetts, according to Forbes
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Forbes' 2026 World's Billionaires List names close to 30 Massachusetts-linked billionaires among 989 U.S. entries.
New inclusions from Massachusetts are Celtics owner Bill Chisholm, GE Aerospace's Larry Culp, and Klaviyo's Andrew Bialecki.
Abigail Johnson leads Massachusetts billionaires with $33.2 billion, followed by Robert Kraft at $13.8 billion.

title: ‘I am a Raider’: Maxx Crosby breaks silence after Ravens trade falls through
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Maxx Crosby's trade to the Baltimore Ravens fell through due to concerns over his medical status.
Crosby remains with the Las Vegas Raiders and addressed the situation in a social media post.
The New England Patriots have been linked as a potential new team for Crosby amid their pass-rushing needs.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her grandmother, support cancer patients
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Laura Davis, 35, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her grandmother, 'Gamma,' and support cancer patients.
Davis is from Wakefield, Massachusetts, and runs for Dana-Farber, inspired by her grandmother's battle with pancreatic cancer.
Since 2019, Davis has completed five marathons, raising funds for cancer research and building a community of supportive runners.

title: Carjacking suspect shot and killed in Roxbury, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston police shot and killed a carjacking suspect in Roxbury after the suspect drove towards officers.
The incident occurred around 9:45 p.m. near 10 Linwood Square; the suspect disregarded officers' commands to stop.
The suspect, unidentified, was pronounced dead at a hospital; officers and carjacking victim were unharmed.

title: Colin Dorgan, who lost 3 family members in Pawtucket shooting, scores game-winning goal
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Colin Dorgan scored the game-winning goal for Blackstone Valley Co-op, securing a 3-2 victory in double overtime.
Dorgan faced personal tragedy after losing his mother, brother, and grandfather in a February shooting at a Pawtucket arena.
The Blackstone Valley team wears hearts on their jerseys to honor Dorgan's family, offering support after the shooting.

title: ‘We are in the midst of winter versus spring here’: Ski resorts are closing operations, reducing lift prices, limiting lifts
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Ski resorts in Massachusetts and Vermont, including Mad River Glenn and Jiminy Peak, closed due to heavy rain and wind.
New Hampshire's Wildcat Mountain adjusted lift prices and operations after warm spells and rain affected snowpack.
Vermont's Jay Peak limited upper mountain lifts due to high winds and freezing trail conditions on Thursday.
